company.name: ArcticRuff Dropship
company.goal:
Build an automated dropshipping business for fur hood replacements (brand: ArcticRuff). The frontend is already deployed at arcticruff-deploy.vercel.app (Next.js 16, Tailwind v4). The business model: customers order on our English site, orders get relayed to Chinese Taobao suppliers who ship directly to buyers internationally. NO inventory held.

BUILD THESE SYSTEMS:

1. ORDER PIPELINE: Customer checkout → order stored in Supabase → auto-formatted into Chinese (supplier speaks NO English) → sent to supplier via WeChat API or email. Include: buyer shipping address, product variant (color/pattern/size), quantity. Order status tracking (placed/confirmed/shipped/delivered).

2. SUPPLIER INVENTORY SYNC: Build a scraper/sync system that pulls product data from Taobao supplier listings. Extract: available colors, patterns, sizes, prices (CNY), stock status, shipping options. Store in Supabase. The storefront should only show in-stock variants. Run sync on a schedule (daily cron or n8n webhook).

3. CHECKOUT & PAYMENTS: Stripe checkout page at /checkout with the 3 tiers ($149/$199/$299 CAD). Collect shipping address, jacket model, fur color/size preference. Store orders in Supabase with payment status.

4. ADMIN DASHBOARD: /admin page showing: all orders with status, supplier inventory snapshot, revenue stats, ability to manually update order status, export to CSV. Basic auth protection.

5. BILINGUAL ORDER RELAY: When an order comes in, auto-generate a Chinese-language order summary (产品/颜色/尺寸/收货地址) formatted for copy-paste into WeChat or auto-send. Include international shipping address transliterated to Chinese format.

6. NOTIFICATION SYSTEM: Slack webhook for new orders + email confirmation to buyer via Resend.

7. SEO + ANALYTICS: Proper meta tags, JSON-LD Product schema, sitemap.xml, robots.txt, GA4 with event tracking.

Tech: Next.js App Router, Supabase (DB + auth), Stripe, Resend, Tailwind CSS v4. All API keys via env vars.

tasks.title: Create Supabase schema with orders and inventory tables
tasks.description:
1. Install dependencies: `npm install @supabase/supabase-js`
2. Create `supabase/migrations/20260318000001_initial_schema.sql` with:
   - `orders` table: id (uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid()), created_at (timestamptz default now()), stripe_payment_id (text unique not null), status (text check in ('placed','confirmed','shipped','delivered') default 'placed'), customer_email (text not null), shipping_address (jsonb not null), product_tier (text not null), fur_color (text), fur_size (text), jacket_model (text), total_cad (numeric(10,2) not null), notes (text)
   - `inventory` table: id (uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid()), taobao_url (text), product_name_zh (text not null), product_name_en (text not null), color (text not null), pattern (text), size (text not null), price_cny (numeric(10,2) not null), stock_available (boolean default true), last_synced_at (timestamptz default now())
   - Indexes: CREATE INDEX idx_orders_status ON orders(status); CREATE INDEX idx_inventory_stock ON inventory(stock_available) WHERE stock_available = true;
   - RLS: ALTER TABLE orders ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY; CREATE POLICY admin_all ON orders FOR ALL USING (auth.role() = 'authenticated'); ALTER TABLE inventory ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY; CREATE POLICY read_inventory ON inventory FOR SELECT USING (true); CREATE POLICY admin_inventory ON inventory FOR ALL USING (auth.role() = 'authenticated');
3. Create `lib/supabase/client.ts` with createClient() for client-side and `lib/supabase/server.ts` with createServerClient() for server-side
4. Add to `.env.local`: NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL, NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY, SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY
5. Run migration via Supabase dashboard SQL editor or CLI
6. Seed inventory table with at least 5 sample fur colors (black, white, grey, brown, tan) and 3 sizes (S, M, L) all marked stock_available=true

tasks.title: Implement /checkout page with Stripe integration and inventory-driven form
tasks.description:
1. Install: `npm install stripe @stripe/stripe-js zod react-hook-form @hookform/resolvers`
2. Create `src/app/checkout/page.tsx`:
   - Fetch available colors from Supabase: SELECT DISTINCT color FROM inventory WHERE stock_available = true
   - Render form with fields: tier (radio: premium_basic=$149, premium_plus=$199, premium_ultimate=$299), shipping (name, address_line1, address_line2, city, province, postal_code, country with Canada default), jacket_model (text input), fur_color (select from fetched colors), fur_size (select S/M/L)
   - Use zod schema for validation: shippingAddressSchema with required name/address_line1/city/province/postal_code/country, optional address_line2
   - On submit, POST to /api/checkout with body: {tier, shipping_address, jacket_model, fur_color, fur_size}
3. Create `src/app/api/checkout/route.ts`:
   - Import Stripe: const stripe = new Stripe(process.env.STRIPE_SECRET_KEY!)
   - Parse request body, validate with zod
   - Create Stripe session: stripe.checkout.sessions.create({mode: 'payment', line_items: [{price_data: {currency: 'cad', unit_amount: tierAmount * 100, product_data: {name: `ArcticRuff ${tier}`, description: `Fur: ${fur_color} ${fur_size}`}}, quantity: 1}], metadata: {tier, jacket_model, fur_color, fur_size, shipping_address: JSON.stringify(shipping_address)}, customer_email: shipping_address.email, success_url: `${process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL}/success?session_id={CHECKOUT_SESSION_ID}`, cancel_url: `${process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL}/checkout`})
   - Return {url: session.url}
4. Add to `.env.local`: STRIPE_SECRET_KEY, NEXT_PUBLIC_STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY, NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL=http://localhost:3000
5. Style form with Tailwind v4, mobile-responsive, loading states during submission
DONE: Form submits, redirects to Stripe checkout, can complete test purchase with card 4242 4242 4242 4242

tasks.title: Build Stripe webhook handler to persist orders in Supabase on payment success
tasks.description:
1. Create `src/app/api/webhooks/stripe/route.ts`:
   - Import: stripe, @supabase/supabase-js (use service role key for server-side writes)
   - Disable bodyParser: export const config = {api: {bodyParser: false}} (WAIT - Next.js 15 App Router doesn't use this, use raw body from request.text())
   - In POST handler: const rawBody = await request.text(); const sig = request.headers.get('stripe-signature')!
   - Verify: const event = stripe.webhooks.constructEvent(rawBody, sig, process.env.STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET!)
   - If event.type === 'checkout.session.completed': extract session = event.data.object; const {tier, jacket_model, fur_color, fur_size, shipping_address} = session.metadata; const shipping = JSON.parse(shipping_address)
   - Insert into Supabase: supabase.from('orders').insert({stripe_payment_id: session.payment_intent, status: 'placed', customer_email: session.customer_email, shipping_address: shipping, product_tier: tier, fur_color, fur_size, jacket_model, total_cad: session.amount_total / 100})
   - Return Response.json({received: true}, {status: 200})
   - Wrap in try/catch, return 400 on signature verification failure
2. Test locally: `stripe listen --forward-to localhost:3000/api/webhooks/stripe` → copy webhook secret to .env.local as STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET
3. Trigger test: `stripe trigger checkout.session.completed`
4. Verify order inserted in Supabase orders table
5. Deploy to Vercel, register webhook endpoint `https://arcticruff-deploy.vercel.app/api/webhooks/stripe` in Stripe dashboard with event checkout.session.completed enabled
DONE: Webhook receives event, verifies signature, inserts order into Supabase with status='placed', returns 200

tasks.title: Build /success page showing order details from Stripe session and Supabase
tasks.description:
1. Create `src/app/success/page.tsx`:
   - Use searchParams to get session_id: const searchParams = await props.searchParams; const sessionId = searchParams.session_id
   - If no sessionId, redirect('/')
   - Server-side fetch Stripe session: const session = await stripe.checkout.sessions.retrieve(sessionId, {expand: ['line_items']})
   - Query Supabase for order: const {data: order} = await supabase.from('orders').select('*').eq('stripe_payment_id', session.payment_intent).single()
   - If order not found (webhook delay), show loading state with meta refresh after 2s OR implement client-side polling
   - Display: Order ID (order.id), Product tier (order.product_tier), Fur details (order.fur_color, order.fur_size), Jacket model (order.jacket_model), Shipping address (formatted from order.shipping_address jsonb), Total paid (order.total_cad CAD), Order status (order.status - should be 'placed'), Estimated delivery: 2-3 weeks
   - Show success message: 'Order confirmed! You'll receive tracking info via email once your order ships.'
   - Style with Tailwind v4: centered card, green checkmark icon, responsive layout
2. Handle edge cases: session not found (show error), order not yet in DB (show 'Processing...' with retry), invalid session_id (redirect home)
3. Add print button to print order summary
DONE: /success?session_id=cs_test_xxx displays order details, shipping address renders correctly from jsonb, shows placed status, styled responsively

tasks.title: Implement Taobao inventory scraper with Playwright and OpenAI translation
tasks.description:
**Files to create:**
- `scripts/taobao-scraper.ts` - Main scraper logic
- `lib/supabase/inventory.ts` - Supabase inventory table CRUD operations
- `types/inventory.ts` - TypeScript types for inventory data

**Dependencies to install:**
```bash
bun add playwright cheerio openai
bun add -d @types/cheerio
```

**Implementation approach:**
1. Initialize Playwright browser (headless: true, viewport: 1920x1080)
2. Navigate to Taobao supplier listing URL (configured in .env as TAOBAO_SUPPLIER_URL)
3. Wait for product grid selector '.item' to load (timeout: 30s)
4. Extract HTML and parse with Cheerio:
   - Product name: `.title` selector
   - Colors: `.color-options .sku-item` data attributes
   - Sizes: `.size-options .sku-item` text content
   - Price: `.price` text, parse CNY float
   - Stock status: check for `.soldout` class or '有货'/'缺货' text
   - Main image: `.main-img img` src attribute (use full resolution)
5. For each product, call OpenAI GPT-4 Turbo to translate Chinese name to English (system prompt: 'Translate this Chinese product name to English for a fur hood product listing. Return only the translation.')
6. Upsert to Supabase `inventory` table:
   - Columns: id (uuid), product_name_zh (text), product_name_en (text), colors (jsonb), sizes (jsonb), price_cny (decimal), in_stock (boolean), image_url (text), last_synced_at (timestamptz)
   - Use `upsert` with conflict resolution on product_name_zh
7. Rate limiting: 2000ms delay between page navigations using `await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 2000))`
8. Pagination: detect `.next-page` button, click and repeat until disabled
9. Console logging: `✓ Synced {count} products | ✗ {errorCount} errors`
10. Close browser and return sync summary object

**Acceptance criteria:**
- ✅ Successfully scrapes at least 10 products from test Taobao URL
- ✅ All product fields populated in Supabase with correct types
- ✅ Chinese names translated to English via OpenAI
- ✅ Rate limiting prevents IP blocking (verified with 50+ product scrape)
- ✅ Script exits cleanly on error with descriptive error message
- ✅ Run via `bun run scripts/taobao-scraper.ts` with no compilation errors

tasks.title: Create bilingual order formatter with OpenAI address transliteration
Build Supabase webhook-triggered supplier email relay API
Deploy Vercel cron for daily Taobao inventory sync at 3AM UTC
Admin Dashboard with Order Management & Auth
tasks.description:
CREATE FILES:
- src/app/admin/page.tsx: Server Component fetching from Supabase tables (orders, product_variants, aggregated revenue). Three sections: (1) Orders table with columns [id, created_at, tier, status enum (pending/confirmed/shipped/delivered), customer_email, total_cad]. Include <select> for status update (calls Server Action updateOrderStatus) + CSV export button (calls generateOrderCSV). (2) Inventory table from product_variants [variant_id, product_name, color, size, stock_status, last_synced_at]. (3) Revenue cards: total orders count, sum(total_cad), breakdown by tier using GROUP BY. (2) src/app/admin/login/page.tsx: Form with password input, Server Action that compares to process.env.ADMIN_PASSWORD, sets httpOnly cookie 'admin_session' with 7-day expiry on success, redirects to /admin. (3) src/middleware.ts: Check if pathname startsWith /admin && pathname !== /admin/login, verify admin_session cookie exists (simple presence check), redirect to /admin/login if missing. (4) src/lib/supabase/admin-queries.ts: getOrders(), getInventory(), getRevenueStats() functions using Supabase client.

DEPENDENCIES: None (use existing @supabase/ssr).

IMPLEMENTATION: Use Server Components for zero-client JS, Server Actions for mutations, cookies() from next/headers for auth, Tailwind tables with hover states. CSV export generates file client-side from fetched data.

DONE CRITERIA: (1) /admin redirects to /admin/login when not authenticated. (2) Login with correct ADMIN_PASSWORD sets cookie and grants access. (3) Orders table displays all rows from Supabase with working status dropdown. (4) CSV export downloads orders.csv. (5) Revenue stats show accurate totals.

tasks.title: Slack + Email Notifications for New Orders
tasks.description:
CREATE FILES:
- src/lib/notify.ts: Export two async functions: (1) sendSlackNotification(order: {id, tier, total_cad, customer_email, created_at}) - POST to process.env.SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL with JSON payload {text: 'New Order #${id}', blocks: [{type: 'section', fields: [{type: 'mrkdwn', text: '*Tier:* ${tier}\n*Total:* $${total_cad} CAD\n*Customer:* ${customer_email}'}]}]}. (2) sendCustomerEmail(order: {id, customer_email, customer_name, tier, total_cad, items: []}) - use Resend SDK to send from noreply@arcticruff.com with OrderConfirmation React Email template.
- emails/OrderConfirmation.tsx: React Email component with ArcticRuff header, 'Thank you for your order!' headline, order details table (Order #, Tier, Total), 'We'll notify you when shipped' message, footer with logo.
- UPDATE src/app/api/webhooks/stripe/route.ts: In checkout.session.completed handler, after inserting order to Supabase, call both await sendSlackNotification(orderData) and await sendCustomerEmail(orderData). Wrap in try-catch to prevent webhook failure if notifications fail.

INSTALL: npm install resend @react-email/components

CONFIG: Add to .env: SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL, RESEND_API_KEY. Verify Resend domain.

DONE CRITERIA: (1) Complete Stripe test checkout → Slack message appears in configured channel with order details. (2) Customer receives email at provided address with order confirmation. (3) Webhook returns 200 even if notification fails (logged error).

tasks.title: GA4 Tracking, SEO Meta Tags, and Static Site Files
tasks.description:
MODIFY FILES:
- src/app/layout.tsx: (1) Add metadata export with openGraph: {title: 'ArcticRuff - Premium Fur Hood Replacements', description: '...', images: ['/og-image.png']}, twitter card. (2) Add <Script> in body with GA4 gtag.js: src=https://www.googletagmanager.com/gtag/js?id=${process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_GA_MEASUREMENT_ID}, strategy='afterInteractive'. (3) Inline <Script> with gtag config.
- src/components/GoogleAnalytics.tsx: Client Component wrapper for gtag event helpers. Export useAnalytics() hook with trackEvent(eventName, params).
- src/app/page.tsx: Add JSON-LD script tag in component: <script type='application/ld+json' dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{__html: JSON.stringify({@context: 'https://schema.org', @type: 'Product', name: 'ArcticRuff Essential Tier', offers: {price: 149, priceCurrency: 'CAD'}})}} /> for each tier. Three separate Product schemas.
- src/app/checkout/page.tsx: Call trackEvent('begin_checkout', {currency: 'CAD', value: selectedTier.price}) on mount.
- src/app/success/page.tsx: Call trackEvent('purchase', {transaction_id: orderId, value: total, currency: 'CAD'}) on mount with query params.

CREATE FILES:
- public/sitemap.xml: Static XML with <url> entries for https://arcticruff-deploy.vercel.app/, /checkout, /success. Include <lastmod> with current date.
- public/robots.txt: 'User-agent: *\nAllow: /\nSitemap: https://arcticruff-deploy.vercel.app/sitemap.xml'
- public/og-image.png: 1200x630 OG image (use existing hero or create in Figma).

AUDIT: Run through all images in components - add alt text where missing.

DONE CRITERIA: (1) Google Tag Assistant shows GA4 firing on homepage. (2) Test checkout triggers 'begin_checkout' event visible in GA4 DebugView. (3) Success page triggers 'purchase'. (4) View page source shows JSON-LD and OpenGraph tags. (5) /sitemap.xml and /robots.txt accessible. (6) Social share preview shows og-image.

tasks.title: Implement A/B testing framework for pricing and messaging
tasks.description: Install Vercel Edge Config + edge middleware for A/B testing. Create /lib/experiments.ts with experiment definitions (price_test_v1: $149/$199/$299 vs $179/$229/$329, hero_cta_test: 'Order Now' vs 'Get Yours Today'). Add experiment tracking to GA4 events. Build /admin/experiments page showing variant performance (conversion rate, revenue per visitor). Store experiment assignments in cookies. Test with 50/50 traffic split on checkout page.

tasks.title: Build abandoned cart recovery email system
tasks.description: Create /api/cart/abandon route that stores incomplete checkouts in Supabase abandoned_carts table (email, tier, timestamp, recovered boolean). Set up Vercel cron at /api/cron/cart-recovery running every 6 hours to send Resend emails to carts abandoned >2 hours ago with discount code COMPLETE10 (10% off). Email template includes product images, one-click return to checkout with pre-filled form. Track recovery conversions in GA4. Expected 15-25% recovery rate.

tasks.title: Deploy production environment with monitoring
tasks.description: Set up Vercel production deployment with custom domain arcticruff.com (configure DNS). Enable Vercel Analytics and Speed Insights. Set up Sentry error tracking (SENTRY_DSN in env). Create /api/health route returning system status (Supabase connection, Stripe API, inventory sync timestamp). Set up UptimeRobot to ping /api/health every 5 minutes, alert via email if down. Configure Vercel log drains to export to Logtail for 30-day retention. Add real Stripe production keys and test full checkout flow.

tasks.title: Build trust signals and social proof system
tasks.description: Create Supabase reviews table (id, order_id foreign key, rating 1-5, review_text, customer_name, verified boolean, created_at, approved boolean). Build /api/reviews/submit POST route for customers to leave reviews after delivery (triggered by email with unique token). Create ReviewsSection component for homepage pulling approved reviews with 4+ stars. Add Trustpilot widget embed. Create /testimonials page with full review list and before/after photos. Add '4.8★ from 127 reviews' to hero section. Include 'As Seen On' section with logo placeholders for press mentions.

tasks.title: Launch Google Ads campaign with conversion tracking
tasks.description: Create Google Ads account and install gtag conversion tracking in app/layout.tsx for purchase events (value: total_cad). Build 3 Search campaigns: (1) Branded ('arctic ruff', 'arcticruff fur'), (2) Category ('fur hood replacement', 'parka fur trim', 'canada goose fur replacement'), (3) Competitor ('moncler fur replacement', 'woolrich hood fur'). Set daily budget $50 CAD, max CPC $2. Create 5 ad variations per campaign testing headlines. Build /landing/google-search page with higher intent CTA and no nav distractions. Track CPA and ROAS in /admin dashboard pulling from GA4 API. Goal: <$30 CAD CPA.

tasks.title: Create SEO content marketing system
tasks.description: Build /blog with MDX support using next-mdx-remote. Create 10 SEO-optimized articles in /content/blog/*.mdx: 'How to Replace Canada Goose Fur', 'Best Faux Fur vs Real Fur Comparison', 'Fur Hood Sizing Guide', 'Winter Jacket Care Guide', 'Arctic Ruff vs Competitor Comparison'. Each article 1500+ words with target keywords, internal links to /checkout, embedded product CTAs. Add schema.org Article markup. Build BlogPostCard component for /blog listing. Submit sitemap to Google Search Console. Install next-sitemap package for auto-generation. Target 50 organic visitors/month by month 3.

tasks.title: Set up Meta Ads (Facebook/Instagram) with dynamic product ads
tasks.description: Create Facebook Business Manager account and install Meta Pixel in app/layout.tsx. Upload product catalog to Facebook with all 3 tiers, images, prices. Set up Conversion API via /api/fb-capi route to send server-side events (ViewContent, AddToCart, Purchase) from checkout flow. Create 2 campaigns: (1) Interest targeting (winter sports, outdoor enthusiasts, luxury fashion) with carousel ads showing before/after fur replacement, (2) Dynamic retargeting showing exact tier viewed. Daily budget $30 CAD. Create 5 video ad variants (15s product showcase). Track CPA and ROAS. Goal: <$40 CAD CPA.

tasks.title: Build referral program for viral growth
tasks.description: Create Supabase referrals table (referrer_email, referee_email, referral_code unique, status enum [pending/completed], created_at, order_id). Add /api/referrals/generate POST route to create unique codes for customers post-purchase. Send email via Resend with referral link: arcticruff.com/checkout?ref=CODE giving referee $20 off and referrer $20 credit on next purchase. Modify checkout to detect ?ref param and apply discount. Build /admin/referrals dashboard showing viral coefficient and top referrers. Email top 5 referrers monthly with exclusive early access to new products. Target 0.3 viral coefficient (30% of customers refer 1 friend).

tasks.title: Implement one-click upsells at checkout
tasks.description: Create upsells table in Supabase (id, name, description, price_cad, image_url, active). Add 3 upsells: (1) 'Fur Care Kit' $29 (brush + spray), (2) 'Extended Warranty' $39 (2-year replacement guarantee), (3) 'Express Shipping' $49 (7-10 days vs 2-3 weeks). Build UpsellsSection component in app/checkout/page.tsx showing after tier selection with checkboxes. Update Stripe checkout session to include upsells as additional line items. Track upsell attachment rate in /admin (goal: 35% take at least one upsell). A/B test placement (before vs after shipping form) and messaging ('Protect Your Investment' vs 'Most Popular Add-On').

tasks.title: Build post-purchase email nurture sequence
tasks.description: Create /api/emails/nurture route triggered by order status changes. Sequence: Day 0 (order confirmed) - order summary + care guide PDF, Day 3 (shipped) - tracking link + installation video, Day 14 (delivered) - how's it look? + review request with $10 off next order, Day 30 - related products email (jacket care products), Day 90 - repurchase trigger (buy for friend/second jacket) with 15% loyalty discount. Use Resend with email templates in /emails/*.tsx using react-email. Store email_sent status in orders table to prevent duplicates. Track open rates and click-through in /admin. Goal: 20% repeat purchase rate within 6 months.

tasks.title: Implement dynamic pricing based on inventory and demand
tasks.description: Create pricing_rules table in Supabase (tier, base_price_cad, current_price_cad, inventory_threshold, demand_multiplier, updated_at). Build /api/pricing/calculate route that adjusts prices based on: (1) Supplier inventory levels (<20 units = +15% surge), (2) Order velocity (>10 orders/day = +10% surge), (3) Seasonal demand (Nov-Feb = peak, +5%). Update checkout page to fetch current prices from API instead of hardcoded $149/$199/$299. Show 'Limited Stock - Price Increasing Soon' banner when surge pricing active. A/B test surge pricing vs static to measure revenue impact. Log all price changes in pricing_history table for analysis. Goal: +8% average order value.

tasks.title: Launch bundle deals and seasonal promotions
tasks.description: Create promotions table in Supabase (code, discount_percent, min_purchase_cad, max_uses, expires_at, active). Build /api/promotions/validate POST route for applying promo codes at checkout. Launch 4 promotions: (1) 'WINTER20' - 20% off $300+ orders (Black Friday/Cyber Monday), (2) 'BUNDLE50' - buy 2 get $50 off (encourage gifting), (3) 'FIRST15' - 15% first-time customer discount (email capture), (4) 'FLASH24' - 24-hour flash sale announced via email list. Create PromoCodeInput component in checkout. Track redemption rates and revenue impact per promo in /admin. Send email blasts via Resend to email list (build from order emails + newsletter signups) for flash sales. Goal: 25% of orders use promo code, +$15K revenue from promotions monthly.

projects.title: Payment & Order Infrastructure
projects.description: Enable customers to purchase fur hood replacements and store orders in a structured database ready for supplier relay

childTasks.title: Create Supabase schema with orders and inventory tables
childTasks.description:
1. Install dependencies: `npm install @supabase/supabase-js`
2. Create `supabase/migrations/20260318000001_initial_schema.sql` with:
   - `orders` table: id (uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid()), created_at (timestamptz default now()), stripe_payment_id (text unique not null), status (text check in ('placed','confirmed','shipped','delivered') default 'placed'), customer_email (text not null), shipping_address (jsonb not null), product_tier (text not null), fur_color (text), fur_size (text), jacket_model (text), total_cad (numeric(10,2) not null), notes (text)
   - `inventory` table: id (uuid primary key default gen_random_uuid()), taobao_url (text), product_name_zh (text not null), product_name_en (text not null), color (text not null), pattern (text), size (text not null), price_cny (numeric(10,2) not null), stock_available (boolean default true), last_synced_at (timestamptz default now())
   - Indexes: CREATE INDEX idx_orders_status ON orders(status); CREATE INDEX idx_inventory_stock ON inventory(stock_available) WHERE stock_available = true;
   - RLS: ALTER TABLE orders ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY; CREATE POLICY admin_all ON orders FOR ALL USING (auth.role() = 'authenticated'); ALTER TABLE inventory ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY; CREATE POLICY read_inventory ON inventory FOR SELECT USING (true); CREATE POLICY admin_inventory ON inventory FOR ALL USING (auth.role() = 'authenticated');
3. Create `lib/supabase/client.ts` with createClient() for client-side and `lib/supabase/server.ts` with createServerClient() for server-side
4. Add to `.env.local`: NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_URL, NEXT_PUBLIC_SUPABASE_ANON_KEY, SUPABASE_SERVICE_ROLE_KEY
5. Run migration via Supabase dashboard SQL editor or CLI
6. Seed inventory table with at least 5 sample fur colors (black, white, grey, brown, tan) and 3 sizes (S, M, L) all marked stock_available=true

childTasks.title: Implement /checkout page with Stripe integration and inventory-driven form
childTasks.description:
1. Install: `npm install stripe @stripe/stripe-js zod react-hook-form @hookform/resolvers`
2. Create `src/app/checkout/page.tsx`:
   - Fetch available colors from Supabase: SELECT DISTINCT color FROM inventory WHERE stock_available = true
   - Render form with fields: tier (radio: premium_basic=$149, premium_plus=$199, premium_ultimate=$299), shipping (name, address_line1, address_line2, city, province, postal_code, country with Canada default), jacket_model (text input), fur_color (select from fetched colors), fur_size (select S/M/L)
   - Use zod schema for validation: shippingAddressSchema with required name/address_line1/city/province/postal_code/country, optional address_line2
   - On submit, POST to /api/checkout with body: {tier, shipping_address, jacket_model, fur_color, fur_size}
3. Create `src/app/api/checkout/route.ts`:
   - Import Stripe: const stripe = new Stripe(process.env.STRIPE_SECRET_KEY!)
   - Parse request body, validate with zod
   - Create Stripe session: stripe.checkout.sessions.create({mode: 'payment', line_items: [{price_data: {currency: 'cad', unit_amount: tierAmount * 100, product_data: {name: `ArcticRuff ${tier}`, description: `Fur: ${fur_color} ${fur_size}`}}, quantity: 1}], metadata: {tier, jacket_model, fur_color, fur_size, shipping_address: JSON.stringify(shipping_address)}, customer_email: shipping_address.email, success_url: `${process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL}/success?session_id={CHECKOUT_SESSION_ID}`, cancel_url: `${process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL}/checkout`})
   - Return {url: session.url}
4. Add to `.env.local`: STRIPE_SECRET_KEY, NEXT_PUBLIC_STRIPE_PUBLISHABLE_KEY, NEXT_PUBLIC_BASE_URL=http://localhost:3000
5. Style form with Tailwind v4, mobile-responsive, loading states during submission
DONE: Form submits, redirects to Stripe checkout, can complete test purchase with card 4242 4242 4242 4242

childTasks.title: Build Stripe webhook handler to persist orders in Supabase on payment success
childTasks.description:
1. Create `src/app/api/webhooks/stripe/route.ts`:
   - Import: stripe, @supabase/supabase-js (use service role key for server-side writes)
   - Disable bodyParser: export const config = {api: {bodyParser: false}} (WAIT - Next.js 15 App Router doesn't use this, use raw body from request.text())
   - In POST handler: const rawBody = await request.text(); const sig = request.headers.get('stripe-signature')!
   - Verify: const event = stripe.webhooks.constructEvent(rawBody, sig, process.env.STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET!)
   - If event.type === 'checkout.session.completed': extract session = event.data.object; const {tier, jacket_model, fur_color, fur_size, shipping_address} = session.metadata; const shipping = JSON.parse(shipping_address)
   - Insert into Supabase: supabase.from('orders').insert({stripe_payment_id: session.payment_intent, status: 'placed', customer_email: session.customer_email, shipping_address: shipping, product_tier: tier, fur_color, fur_size, jacket_model, total_cad: session.amount_total / 100})
   - Return Response.json({received: true}, {status: 200})
   - Wrap in try/catch, return 400 on signature verification failure
2. Test locally: `stripe listen --forward-to localhost:3000/api/webhooks/stripe` → copy webhook secret to .env.local as STRIPE_WEBHOOK_SECRET
3. Trigger test: `stripe trigger checkout.session.completed`
4. Verify order inserted in Supabase orders table
5. Deploy to Vercel, register webhook endpoint `https://arcticruff-deploy.vercel.app/api/webhooks/stripe` in Stripe dashboard with event checkout.session.completed enabled
DONE: Webhook receives event, verifies signature, inserts order into Supabase with status='placed', returns 200

childTasks.title: Build /success page showing order details from Stripe session and Supabase
childTasks.description:
1. Create `src/app/success/page.tsx`:
   - Use searchParams to get session_id: const searchParams = await props.searchParams; const sessionId = searchParams.session_id
   - If no sessionId, redirect('/')
   - Server-side fetch Stripe session: const session = await stripe.checkout.sessions.retrieve(sessionId, {expand: ['line_items']})
   - Query Supabase for order: const {data: order} = await supabase.from('orders').select('*').eq('stripe_payment_id', session.payment_intent).single()
   - If order not found (webhook delay), show loading state with meta refresh after 2s OR implement client-side polling
   - Display: Order ID (order.id), Product tier (order.product_tier), Fur details (order.fur_color, order.fur_size), Jacket model (order.jacket_model), Shipping address (formatted from order.shipping_address jsonb), Total paid (order.total_cad CAD), Order status (order.status - should be 'placed'), Estimated delivery: 2-3 weeks
   - Show success message: 'Order confirmed! You'll receive tracking info via email once your order ships.'
   - Style with Tailwind v4: centered card, green checkmark icon, responsive layout
2. Handle edge cases: session not found (show error), order not yet in DB (show 'Processing...' with retry), invalid session_id (redirect home)
3. Add print button to print order summary
DONE: /success?session_id=cs_test_xxx displays order details, shipping address renders correctly from jsonb, shows placed status, styled responsively

projects.title: Supplier Integration & Automation
projects.description: Sync inventory from Taobao suppliers and auto-relay orders in Chinese format for seamless fulfillment

childTasks.title: Implement Taobao inventory scraper with Playwright and OpenAI translation
childTasks.description:
**Files to create:**
- `scripts/taobao-scraper.ts` - Main scraper logic
- `lib/supabase/inventory.ts` - Supabase inventory table CRUD operations
- `types/inventory.ts` - TypeScript types for inventory data

**Dependencies to install:**
```bash
bun add playwright cheerio openai
bun add -d @types/cheerio
```

**Implementation approach:**
1. Initialize Playwright browser (headless: true, viewport: 1920x1080)
2. Navigate to Taobao supplier listing URL (configured in .env as TAOBAO_SUPPLIER_URL)
3. Wait for product grid selector '.item' to load (timeout: 30s)
4. Extract HTML and parse with Cheerio:
   - Product name: `.title` selector
   - Colors: `.color-options .sku-item` data attributes
   - Sizes: `.size-options .sku-item` text content
   - Price: `.price` text, parse CNY float
   - Stock status: check for `.soldout` class or '有货'/'缺货' text
   - Main image: `.main-img img` src attribute (use full resolution)
5. For each product, call OpenAI GPT-4 Turbo to translate Chinese name to English (system prompt: 'Translate this Chinese product name to English for a fur hood product listing. Return only the translation.')
6. Upsert to Supabase `inventory` table:
   - Columns: id (uuid), product_name_zh (text), product_name_en (text), colors (jsonb), sizes (jsonb), price_cny (decimal), in_stock (boolean), image_url (text), last_synced_at (timestamptz)
   - Use `upsert` with conflict resolution on product_name_zh
7. Rate limiting: 2000ms delay between page navigations using `await new Promise(r => setTimeout(r, 2000))`
8. Pagination: detect `.next-page` button, click and repeat until disabled
9. Console logging: `✓ Synced {count} products | ✗ {errorCount} errors`
10. Close browser and return sync summary object

**Acceptance criteria:**
- ✅ Successfully scrapes at least 10 products from test Taobao URL
- ✅ All product fields populated in Supabase with correct types
- ✅ Chinese names translated to English via OpenAI
- ✅ Rate limiting prevents IP blocking (verified with 50+ product scrape)
- ✅ Script exits cleanly on error with descriptive error message
- ✅ Run via `bun run scripts/taobao-scraper.ts` with no compilation errors

childTasks.title: Create bilingual order formatter with OpenAI address transliteration
Build Supabase webhook-triggered supplier email relay API
Deploy Vercel cron for daily Taobao inventory sync at 3AM UTC

projects.title: Operations & Analytics
projects.description: Deploy admin dashboard for order management and implement notification + analytics systems for business monitoring

childTasks.title: Admin Dashboard with Order Management & Auth
childTasks.description:
CREATE FILES:
- src/app/admin/page.tsx: Server Component fetching from Supabase tables (orders, product_variants, aggregated revenue). Three sections: (1) Orders table with columns [id, created_at, tier, status enum (pending/confirmed/shipped/delivered), customer_email, total_cad]. Include <select> for status update (calls Server Action updateOrderStatus) + CSV export button (calls generateOrderCSV). (2) Inventory table from product_variants [variant_id, product_name, color, size, stock_status, last_synced_at]. (3) Revenue cards: total orders count, sum(total_cad), breakdown by tier using GROUP BY. (2) src/app/admin/login/page.tsx: Form with password input, Server Action that compares to process.env.ADMIN_PASSWORD, sets httpOnly cookie 'admin_session' with 7-day expiry on success, redirects to /admin. (3) src/middleware.ts: Check if pathname startsWith /admin && pathname !== /admin/login, verify admin_session cookie exists (simple presence check), redirect to /admin/login if missing. (4) src/lib/supabase/admin-queries.ts: getOrders(), getInventory(), getRevenueStats() functions using Supabase client.

DEPENDENCIES: None (use existing @supabase/ssr).

IMPLEMENTATION: Use Server Components for zero-client JS, Server Actions for mutations, cookies() from next/headers for auth, Tailwind tables with hover states. CSV export generates file client-side from fetched data.

DONE CRITERIA: (1) /admin redirects to /admin/login when not authenticated. (2) Login with correct ADMIN_PASSWORD sets cookie and grants access. (3) Orders table displays all rows from Supabase with working status dropdown. (4) CSV export downloads orders.csv. (5) Revenue stats show accurate totals.

childTasks.title: Slack + Email Notifications for New Orders
childTasks.description:
CREATE FILES:
- src/lib/notify.ts: Export two async functions: (1) sendSlackNotification(order: {id, tier, total_cad, customer_email, created_at}) - POST to process.env.SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL with JSON payload {text: 'New Order #${id}', blocks: [{type: 'section', fields: [{type: 'mrkdwn', text: '*Tier:* ${tier}\n*Total:* $${total_cad} CAD\n*Customer:* ${customer_email}'}]}]}. (2) sendCustomerEmail(order: {id, customer_email, customer_name, tier, total_cad, items: []}) - use Resend SDK to send from noreply@arcticruff.com with OrderConfirmation React Email template.
- emails/OrderConfirmation.tsx: React Email component with ArcticRuff header, 'Thank you for your order!' headline, order details table (Order #, Tier, Total), 'We'll notify you when shipped' message, footer with logo.
- UPDATE src/app/api/webhooks/stripe/route.ts: In checkout.session.completed handler, after inserting order to Supabase, call both await sendSlackNotification(orderData) and await sendCustomerEmail(orderData). Wrap in try-catch to prevent webhook failure if notifications fail.

INSTALL: npm install resend @react-email/components

CONFIG: Add to .env: SLACK_WEBHOOK_URL, RESEND_API_KEY. Verify Resend domain.

DONE CRITERIA: (1) Complete Stripe test checkout → Slack message appears in configured channel with order details. (2) Customer receives email at provided address with order confirmation. (3) Webhook returns 200 even if notification fails (logged error).

childTasks.title: GA4 Tracking, SEO Meta Tags, and Static Site Files
childTasks.description:
MODIFY FILES:
- src/app/layout.tsx: (1) Add metadata export with openGraph: {title: 'ArcticRuff - Premium Fur Hood Replacements', description: '...', images: ['/og-image.png']}, twitter card. (2) Add <Script> in body with GA4 gtag.js: src=https://www.googletagmanager.com/gtag/js?id=${process.env.NEXT_PUBLIC_GA_MEASUREMENT_ID}, strategy='afterInteractive'. (3) Inline <Script> with gtag config.
- src/components/GoogleAnalytics.tsx: Client Component wrapper for gtag event helpers. Export useAnalytics() hook with trackEvent(eventName, params).
- src/app/page.tsx: Add JSON-LD script tag in component: <script type='application/ld+json' dangerouslySetInnerHTML={{__html: JSON.stringify({@context: 'https://schema.org', @type: 'Product', name: 'ArcticRuff Essential Tier', offers: {price: 149, priceCurrency: 'CAD'}})}} /> for each tier. Three separate Product schemas.
- src/app/checkout/page.tsx: Call trackEvent('begin_checkout', {currency: 'CAD', value: selectedTier.price}) on mount.
- src/app/success/page.tsx: Call trackEvent('purchase', {transaction_id: orderId, value: total, currency: 'CAD'}) on mount with query params.

CREATE FILES:
- public/sitemap.xml: Static XML with <url> entries for https://arcticruff-deploy.vercel.app/, /checkout, /success. Include <lastmod> with current date.
- public/robots.txt: 'User-agent: *\nAllow: /\nSitemap: https://arcticruff-deploy.vercel.app/sitemap.xml'
- public/og-image.png: 1200x630 OG image (use existing hero or create in Figma).

AUDIT: Run through all images in components - add alt text where missing.

DONE CRITERIA: (1) Google Tag Assistant shows GA4 firing on homepage. (2) Test checkout triggers 'begin_checkout' event visible in GA4 DebugView. (3) Success page triggers 'purchase'. (4) View page source shows JSON-LD and OpenGraph tags. (5) /sitemap.xml and /robots.txt accessible. (6) Social share preview shows og-image.

projects.title: Launch Readiness & Conversion Optimization
projects.description: Polish the site for production launch, implement conversion optimization systems, and deploy monitoring infrastructure to maximize checkout completion rates.

childTasks.title: Implement A/B testing framework for pricing and messaging
childTasks.description: Install Vercel Edge Config + edge middleware for A/B testing. Create /lib/experiments.ts with experiment definitions (price_test_v1: $149/$199/$299 vs $179/$229/$329, hero_cta_test: 'Order Now' vs 'Get Yours Today'). Add experiment tracking to GA4 events. Build /admin/experiments page showing variant performance (conversion rate, revenue per visitor). Store experiment assignments in cookies. Test with 50/50 traffic split on checkout page.

childTasks.title: Build abandoned cart recovery email system
childTasks.description: Create /api/cart/abandon route that stores incomplete checkouts in Supabase abandoned_carts table (email, tier, timestamp, recovered boolean). Set up Vercel cron at /api/cron/cart-recovery running every 6 hours to send Resend emails to carts abandoned >2 hours ago with discount code COMPLETE10 (10% off). Email template includes product images, one-click return to checkout with pre-filled form. Track recovery conversions in GA4. Expected 15-25% recovery rate.

childTasks.title: Deploy production environment with monitoring
childTasks.description: Set up Vercel production deployment with custom domain arcticruff.com (configure DNS). Enable Vercel Analytics and Speed Insights. Set up Sentry error tracking (SENTRY_DSN in env). Create /api/health route returning system status (Supabase connection, Stripe API, inventory sync timestamp). Set up UptimeRobot to ping /api/health every 5 minutes, alert via email if down. Configure Vercel log drains to export to Logtail for 30-day retention. Add real Stripe production keys and test full checkout flow.

childTasks.title: Build trust signals and social proof system
childTasks.description: Create Supabase reviews table (id, order_id foreign key, rating 1-5, review_text, customer_name, verified boolean, created_at, approved boolean). Build /api/reviews/submit POST route for customers to leave reviews after delivery (triggered by email with unique token). Create ReviewsSection component for homepage pulling approved reviews with 4+ stars. Add Trustpilot widget embed. Create /testimonials page with full review list and before/after photos. Add '4.8★ from 127 reviews' to hero section. Include 'As Seen On' section with logo placeholders for press mentions.

projects.title: Customer Acquisition Engine
projects.description: Build scalable paid and organic traffic channels to drive qualified visitors. Target 1000 site visitors/month in month 1, scaling to 10,000/month by month 6.

childTasks.title: Launch Google Ads campaign with conversion tracking
childTasks.description: Create Google Ads account and install gtag conversion tracking in app/layout.tsx for purchase events (value: total_cad). Build 3 Search campaigns: (1) Branded ('arctic ruff', 'arcticruff fur'), (2) Category ('fur hood replacement', 'parka fur trim', 'canada goose fur replacement'), (3) Competitor ('moncler fur replacement', 'woolrich hood fur'). Set daily budget $50 CAD, max CPC $2. Create 5 ad variations per campaign testing headlines. Build /landing/google-search page with higher intent CTA and no nav distractions. Track CPA and ROAS in /admin dashboard pulling from GA4 API. Goal: <$30 CAD CPA.

childTasks.title: Create SEO content marketing system
childTasks.description: Build /blog with MDX support using next-mdx-remote. Create 10 SEO-optimized articles in /content/blog/*.mdx: 'How to Replace Canada Goose Fur', 'Best Faux Fur vs Real Fur Comparison', 'Fur Hood Sizing Guide', 'Winter Jacket Care Guide', 'Arctic Ruff vs Competitor Comparison'. Each article 1500+ words with target keywords, internal links to /checkout, embedded product CTAs. Add schema.org Article markup. Build BlogPostCard component for /blog listing. Submit sitemap to Google Search Console. Install next-sitemap package for auto-generation. Target 50 organic visitors/month by month 3.

childTasks.title: Set up Meta Ads (Facebook/Instagram) with dynamic product ads
childTasks.description: Create Facebook Business Manager account and install Meta Pixel in app/layout.tsx. Upload product catalog to Facebook with all 3 tiers, images, prices. Set up Conversion API via /api/fb-capi route to send server-side events (ViewContent, AddToCart, Purchase) from checkout flow. Create 2 campaigns: (1) Interest targeting (winter sports, outdoor enthusiasts, luxury fashion) with carousel ads showing before/after fur replacement, (2) Dynamic retargeting showing exact tier viewed. Daily budget $30 CAD. Create 5 video ad variants (15s product showcase). Track CPA and ROAS. Goal: <$40 CAD CPA.

childTasks.title: Build referral program for viral growth
childTasks.description: Create Supabase referrals table (referrer_email, referee_email, referral_code unique, status enum [pending/completed], created_at, order_id). Add /api/referrals/generate POST route to create unique codes for customers post-purchase. Send email via Resend with referral link: arcticruff.com/checkout?ref=CODE giving referee $20 off and referrer $20 credit on next purchase. Modify checkout to detect ?ref param and apply discount. Build /admin/referrals dashboard showing viral coefficient and top referrers. Email top 5 referrers monthly with exclusive early access to new products. Target 0.3 viral coefficient (30% of customers refer 1 friend).

projects.title: Revenue Optimization & LTV Maximization
projects.description: Maximize revenue per customer through pricing experiments, upsells, and email marketing automation. Target $250 average order value and 1.5 purchases per customer lifetime.

childTasks.title: Implement one-click upsells at checkout
childTasks.description: Create upsells table in Supabase (id, name, description, price_cad, image_url, active). Add 3 upsells: (1) 'Fur Care Kit' $29 (brush + spray), (2) 'Extended Warranty' $39 (2-year replacement guarantee), (3) 'Express Shipping' $49 (7-10 days vs 2-3 weeks). Build UpsellsSection component in app/checkout/page.tsx showing after tier selection with checkboxes. Update Stripe checkout session to include upsells as additional line items. Track upsell attachment rate in /admin (goal: 35% take at least one upsell). A/B test placement (before vs after shipping form) and messaging ('Protect Your Investment' vs 'Most Popular Add-On').

childTasks.title: Build post-purchase email nurture sequence
childTasks.description: Create /api/emails/nurture route triggered by order status changes. Sequence: Day 0 (order confirmed) - order summary + care guide PDF, Day 3 (shipped) - tracking link + installation video, Day 14 (delivered) - how's it look? + review request with $10 off next order, Day 30 - related products email (jacket care products), Day 90 - repurchase trigger (buy for friend/second jacket) with 15% loyalty discount. Use Resend with email templates in /emails/*.tsx using react-email. Store email_sent status in orders table to prevent duplicates. Track open rates and click-through in /admin. Goal: 20% repeat purchase rate within 6 months.

childTasks.title: Implement dynamic pricing based on inventory and demand
childTasks.description: Create pricing_rules table in Supabase (tier, base_price_cad, current_price_cad, inventory_threshold, demand_multiplier, updated_at). Build /api/pricing/calculate route that adjusts prices based on: (1) Supplier inventory levels (<20 units = +15% surge), (2) Order velocity (>10 orders/day = +10% surge), (3) Seasonal demand (Nov-Feb = peak, +5%). Update checkout page to fetch current prices from API instead of hardcoded $149/$199/$299. Show 'Limited Stock - Price Increasing Soon' banner when surge pricing active. A/B test surge pricing vs static to measure revenue impact. Log all price changes in pricing_history table for analysis. Goal: +8% average order value.

childTasks.title: Launch bundle deals and seasonal promotions
childTasks.description: Create promotions table in Supabase (code, discount_percent, min_purchase_cad, max_uses, expires_at, active). Build /api/promotions/validate POST route for applying promo codes at checkout. Launch 4 promotions: (1) 'WINTER20' - 20% off $300+ orders (Black Friday/Cyber Monday), (2) 'BUNDLE50' - buy 2 get $50 off (encourage gifting), (3) 'FIRST15' - 15% first-time customer discount (email capture), (4) 'FLASH24' - 24-hour flash sale announced via email list. Create PromoCodeInput component in checkout. Track redemption rates and revenue impact per promo in /admin. Send email blasts via Resend to email list (build from order emails + newsletter signups) for flash sales. Goal: 25% of orders use promo code, +$15K revenue from promotions monthly.
